texto: The leave zipped right by. We were so terrifically glad to be back to our own little section of the trench, with all its happy memories, that we wouldn’t have traded places with anybody. The lazy bastard who’d filled in while we were away hadn’t managed to nibble away so much as an inch of garden soil in the direction of Berlin.We found out that Brugnon hadn’t come back from leave. He’d hanged himself in the stairwell of his building, on rue des Gâtines. He left a note to say he couldn’t take it any more and asked us to count him out. We accepted it… Who were we to judge?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Jacques Tardi que provoca temas ligados a felicidade. Ao questionar “The leave zipped right by. We were so terrifically glad to be back to our own little section of t…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre felicidade, suicide, war, com foco em war e ww1.

texto: Moreover, man carries in his heart the desire always to wield his scientific knowledge in service of the greater good. He would of course never use it for destructive purposes. Ha! Ha! Ha! Ha! ...
explicacao: Citação de Jacques Tardi sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Moreover, man carries in his heart the desire always to wield his scientific knowledge in service…” articula amor, dark-humor, dark-humour em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como reflexao-noturna e dark-humour.
